useful_deltas.summary: The shared PCS entry-filter boundary now supports lagged ret_5d, ret_14d, and ema_stack bounds and fails closed rather than raising on malformed configured numeric features.

A predeclared 21-DTE multi-horizon trend-pullback PCS produced four of 24 train-gate passes but zero complete untouched-holdout folds across eight symbols under both proxy cost axes, so the family is closed without tuning, registration, or promotion.

critic_findings.finding: The trend-pullback module lacked a local passing-holdout/failing-train negative control even though sibling rolling-origin labs carry one.
critic_findings.rationale: The local suite now constructs passing holdout axes and a failing train slip axis and proves _fold_pass rejects the fold, preserving the conjunctive train-before-holdout boundary next to the new lab.

critic_findings.finding: Four train gates indicate an almost-edge that should trigger threshold, DTE, or management retuning.
critic_findings.rationale: The predeclared decision required every train-first fold to survive untouched holdouts under both cost axes; zero of 24 complete folds passed. Retuning on the same synthetic marks after seeing holdout failures would be holdout-adjacent search and thrash.

critic_findings.finding: The prior observed-archive NEXT had to be executed on Sunday instead of being superseded.
critic_findings.rationale: Archive density advances only on a distinct New York RTH market date, which Sunday cannot provide. The independent historical-underlying proxy and simulator-capability routes remained executable under explicit L0 claim limits.

critic_findings.finding: The 286 integrity-summary claim is incomplete because a broader nested-field count is larger.
critic_findings.rationale: The established run-summary convention counts dictionaries carrying both integrity and n_trades; the exact rerun contains 286 such summaries and all 286 are true. Nested holdout-window fields are also exact, with aggregate signal and same-bar-reentry violations both zero.

critic_findings.finding: Missing the local fold-gate negative control invalidated the rejection decision.
critic_findings.rationale: The shared _fold_pass implementation was already conjunctive and produced zero complete folds despite four train passes, so the omission was not claim-invalidating. The finalizer nevertheless repaired the local test-parity gap and reconfirmed the complete suite.

critic_findings.finding: The current run's orientation.json should be rewritten to include the newly closed family.
critic_findings.rationale: orientation.json is immutable pre-choice context generated only from prior integrated compounding records. Rewriting it with the current outcome would create temporal leakage; this run records the closure in compounding.json, and the next integrated orientation will ingest it.

data_dependencies: A reliable dividend/ex-date event source and deterministic as-of semantics are required before short-call diagonal/debit assignment-risk claims; dependent simulations must fail closed when event data is absent.

A distinct New York RTH market date is required to advance the TSLL option archive from 1/3 to 2/3; provider-backed historical replay and L1 remain blocked before sufficient history and complete joins.
next: Build a no-lookahead dividend/ex-date and early-assignment risk boundary for short-call diagonal and debit simulators, with deterministic behavioral and negative-control tests before strategy retuning; fail closed when required event data is absent, keep proxy claims L0, and do not reopen the rejected multi-horizon trend-pullback PCS family through threshold polish.
